small.name: Typical 2-Bay Volunteer Station
small.notes: 2 apparatus bays; day room; kitchen; bunk area; single bathroom; office

medium.name: Seattle Fire Station 32
medium.notes: Miller Hull; 3 bays; LEED Gold; bunk rooms; training tower; community room; decon

large.name: FDNY Rescue Company 1
large.notes: 5 bays; specialized rescue; HAZMAT storage; fitness; training; multiple bunk rooms; command center

space_library.name: Apparatus Bay (Drive-Through)
notes: 14 ft wide x 60-80 ft deep per bay; 14 ft clear min door height; drive-through preferred; exhaust extraction system; trench drains; non-slip epoxy floor; 100 PSF floor load
Corrected from S-1 to S-2; enclosed parking areas for apparatus/vehicles are typically Group S-2.

space_library.name: Watch Room / Dispatch Area
notes: Sightline to apparatus bay and entry; dispatch console; alarm notification; CCTV monitors; always staffed
Dispatch/watch functions are more consistent with Group B operational space in mixed-use fire stations.

space_library.name: Day Room / Living Area
notes: Recliners; TV; transition to residential character; acoustic separation from apparatus bay; 35-50 SF per on-duty member

space_library.name: Kitchen / Dining
notes: Full residential kitchen; dining table for crew; 6-person min; commercial-grade appliances; exhaust hood; adjacent to day room

space_library.name: Bunk Rooms
notes: Individual rooms preferred over open dorm; 80-120 SF per person; sound attenuation STC 45+; blackout shades; emergency alerting; near bathrooms; gender-inclusive design

space_library.name: Bathrooms / Showers
notes: Separate from decon; 1 shower per 4 firefighters; gender-inclusive; residential-grade fixtures

space_library.name: Fitness / Physical Training Room
notes: Cardio; free weights; functional training; rubber floor; natural light preferred; ventilation per ASHRAE; 50 SF per station

space_library.name: Decontamination Room
notes: Between apparatus bay and living quarters; extractor washer; gear drying; shower; dirty-to-clean flow; cancer prevention; NFPA 1851

space_library.name: Turnout Gear Storage
notes: Individual lockers; open-front for quick access; ventilated; near apparatus bay; SCBA fill station adjacent; 15-20 SF per firefighter

space_library.name: EMS Supply / Medical Storage
notes: Narcotics lock; crash cart parking; bio-waste; resupply from ambulance; climate-controlled medications

space_library.name: Station Officer / Admin Office
notes: Captain/lieutenant office; records; computer; privacy for disciplinary or counseling; near watch room

space_library.name: Training Room / Community Room
notes: Classroom setup; projector; doubled as community room; public access possible; 15 SF/person; divisible

space_library.name: Hose Tower / Training Tower
notes: 40-60 ft tall; hose drying; rappelling; ladder training; exterior access; fire-resistant construction

space_library.name: Laundry (Residential + Gear)
notes: Separate from gear extractor washer in decon; residential laundry for crew personal items

space_library.name: General Storage / Supply
notes: Hose; nozzles; tools; PPE inventory; seasonal equipment

space_library.name: Mechanical Room
notes: Emergency generator; apparatus bay heating (radiant preferred); exhaust system controls; fire alarm panel

space_library.name: Electrical Room
notes: Transfer switch; emergency power; shore power for apparatus; charging stations

space_library.name: Communications / IT Room
notes: Radio equipment; CAD terminals; antenna infrastructure; UPS; alarm receiving equipment

plan_archetypes.notes: Bar with apparatus bays on one end, living quarters on other
